iterations.title: Capture tredl direction, feedback cycle, and the unverified premises to confirm in source
Record the L0-L5 backlog as ordered plan items, L5 deferred/discovery-triggered
Inventory the existing primitives (L0): on-disk artifact shapes and which verbs read vs write them
P3/L2 keystone (discovery): confirm DoD is write-only and design machine-evaluable DoD checks read at accept
P3/L2 planning: resolve the surface, command-execution, audit, and error-shape forks into an ordered build plan
P7: machine-verifiable DoD - optional 'check' field in iteration_content schema + pure evaluate_dod evaluator (+unit tests)
P8: wire evaluate_dod into transition() as the accept veto - refuse accept on any failed check (+tests)
P9: 'lume check' dry-run verb + verifiability status on 'lume get' (+CLI/tests)
P10: document machine-verifiable DoD + closeout retro for the P3/L2 keystone
P5/L4 discovery: audit delivery gaps - CI, packaging/pyproject, versioning, tagged release, plugin install path
P5/L4 planning: resolve CI/version-tag/release/install-verification forks into an ordered delivery build plan
P11: pyproject.toml (src-layout) + version-consistency check vs plugin.json (+tests)
P12: GitHub Actions CI - pytest matrix 3.11-3.13 + version-consistency check on push/PR
P13: tag + release process - tag==plugin.json version check + documented release flow
P14: install-verification doc (live /plugin install verified in tredl) + P5/L4 closeout retro
P5 hotfix: CI must install pytest (test tooling); strengthen check_ci_workflow to require the install step
P2/L1 discovery: design the cross-repo capability-gap record schema + ADOPTERS scan/ingest, and resolve the 'discovery' naming collision
P2/L1 planning: resolve dir-name, landing store, worktree cache/lifecycle, status transitions, round-trip scope into an ordered build plan
P15: gap entity schema + lume gap add/list (gaps/<id>.json capture) (+tests)
P16: ADOPTERS.md parser + git clone/fetch/worktree reach to read adopter gaps/ (+tests)
P17: 'lume gap scan' - iterate adopters, ingest open gaps as acknowledged, idempotent on (source,id) (+contract test)
P18: docs (gap add/scan/list/resolve) + wire render_adopters --check into CI + P2/L1 closeout retro
P4/L3 discovery: design parent/child workstreams - schema parent ref, spawn verb, rollup, close-guard
P4/L3 planning: resolve verb-shape, rollup depth, close-guard, reopen forks into an ordered build plan
P19: workstream parent ref + lume spawn (child of -w target) + children-by-scan (+tests)
P20: rollup - parent detail lists children+phase; queue nests children under parents (+tests)

plan.sketch: L0 (order 1) - Inventory the existing primitives. OBJECTIVE: establish ground truth on what seed/workstream/iteration/discovery/plan/decide/snapshot already do and what their JSON artifacts actually contain on disk, before extending anything. DONE-WHEN: a short inventory note exists documenting, per primitive, the on-disk artifact shape, which verb(s) read vs only write it, and the current iteration type/phase enums; the gaps assumed in L1-L5 are each confirmed true or struck. ITERATIONS: discovery (read the modules) + closeout (write the inventory note).

L1 (order 2) - Promote discoveries to a first-class, cross-repo mechanism. OBJECTIVE: extend 'discovery' (today an in-repo iteration type + discovery.json schema) into a capture-and-scan mechanism: discoveries/*.json records in any repo, captured from lume's own operator feedback AND from adopter repos, that lume can scan (via ADOPTERS) and pull into its backlog. DONE-WHEN: (1) documented discoveries/*.json schema with stable fields (id, source repo, title, context, status open/acknowledged/resolved, optional resolution link); (2) lume can record a discovery on itself; (3) lume can scan ADOPTERS repos, ingest open discoveries, and not double-ingest on rerun; (4) a contract test covers ingest + dedupe + status transitions. This is the integration API between lume and every adopter - design it deliberately. ITERATIONS: discovery, planning, execution, closeout.

L2 (order 3, KEYSTONE) - Make the Definition of Done machine-evaluable and read at accept. OBJECTIVE: assessment indicates dod_artifact is persisted but not evaluated by any verb (write-only); for an autonomous operator the accept gate is only meaningful if the DoD can be CHECKED, not just asserted. Make DoD support machine-verifiable checks and have accept evaluate them. DONE-WHEN: (1) discovery confirms or refutes the 'DoD is write-only' claim [UNVERIFIED PREMISE A]; (2) a DoD can carry verifiable checks (command-exit-zero / file-exists / schema-valid predicates) alongside prose; (3) lume accept evaluates the verifiable checks and refuses if any fail; (4) prose-only DoD is flagged non-verifiable so a caller can route it to human escalation; (5) tests cover pass, fail, and non-verifiable paths. ITERATIONS: discovery, planning, execution, closeout.

L3 (order 4) - Hierarchical workstreams (a workstream that spawns workstreams). OBJECTIVE: the workstream schema has no parent reference, so a 'sprint' (a workstream that decomposes into and spawns child workstreams) is not representable. Add parent/child relationships and verb support to spawn and roll up children. DONE-WHEN: (1) workstream schema gains an optional parent reference and children are discoverable from a parent [confirms UNVERIFIED PREMISE B in its discovery]; (2) a verb spawns a child workstream linked to its parent; (3) status/snapshot can roll up child state to the parent; (4) closing a parent is refused while children are open (or defines explicit behaviour); (5) tests cover spawn, rollup, and the close guard. ITERATIONS: discovery, planning, execution, closeout.

L4 (order 5) - Delivery practices: CI, releases, packaging, install verification. OBJECTIVE: lume has a strong test suite but no CI, no tagged release, and no packaging despite a v0.1.0 label; an adopter (tredl) cannot pin a dependable version. Establish first-class delivery so lume ships verifiable, pinnable releases. DONE-WHEN: (1) CI runs the full pytest suite on push/PR and is required to pass; (2) a pyproject declares the package and version; (3) a tagged, semver release exists and matches the declared version; (4) the Claude Code plugin install path is verified end to end (documented steps reproduced from a clean state); (5) a release produces an artifact an adopter can pin to. ITERATIONS: discovery (audit current gaps), planning, execution, closeout.

L5 (order 6, DEFERRED / discovery-triggered - DO NOT SCHEDULE) - Declarative workstream/phase pattern instantiation. OBJECTIVE: lume's planned operator-selectable workstream/phase templates. SPLIT: lume owns the MECHANISM to instantiate a workstream/phase structure from a declarative pattern definition; the INTELLIGENCE of choosing/authoring a pattern lives in the consumer (tredl). A custom pattern is tredl's no-lume-code escape hatch for composition gaps. STATUS: deferred on purpose. Do NOT schedule. TRIGGER is empirical: when tredl's ad-hoc custom patterns recur, they arrive as discoveries that justify promoting the mechanism. Build the mechanism (one) when demanded; grow any built-in template library by demonstrated need only, never speculatively (a speculative template library is the scope-creep trap to avoid). DONE-WHEN (when triggered): (1) a documented declarative pattern definition format; (2) a verb instantiates a workstream/phase structure from a pattern; (3) at least one pattern promoted from a real recurring tredl workaround; (4) tests cover instantiation and validation. ITERATIONS (when triggered): discovery, planning, execution, closeout.

P3/L2 build step 1 of 4 - SCHEMA + EVALUATOR. Add an optional 'check' object to each dod item in schemas/iteration_content.json (kinds: command{cmd}, file-exists{path}, schema-valid{entity,path}) and implement a pure evaluator (e.g. plugin/src/lume/dod_checks.py) evaluate_dod(content, repo_root)->per-item results {verifiable, passed, reason}. DONE-WHEN: schema accepts an item with/without check and existing docs still validate; evaluator handles all three kinds incl. failed-to-run; unit tests cover pass/fail/non-verifiable/missing-file/nonzero-exit/bad-schema. ITERATION: execution.

P3/L2 build step 2 of 4 - ACCEPT VETO. Wire the evaluator into Workstream.transition(): on verb=='accept', before the phase mutation, evaluate the current iteration's DoD checks and raise GateError (per FORK 4 shape) if any fail/fail-to-run. DONE-WHEN: accept refuses with exit 1 + structured --json failures when a check fails; accept proceeds when all checks pass or none exist; prose-only DoDs accept exactly as today; tests cover refuse/pass/prose-only/back-compat. ITERATION: execution.

P3/L2 build step 3 of 4 - DRY-RUN + STATUS SURFACE. Add a 'lume check' verb that runs the evaluator without transitioning and prints per-item pass/fail + verifiable/prose-only, and surface verifiability status (and command-check presence flag) on 'lume get'. DONE-WHEN: 'lume check' exits 0/non-zero by aggregate result, --json carries results; 'lume get' exposes the status; CLI catalog/help/usage updated; tests cover the verb + status. ITERATION: execution.

P3/L2 build step 4 of 4 - DOCS + CLOSEOUT. Document machine-verifiable DoD (README + lume skill), assess the objective done-when for the keystone, log decisions, and run the closeout retro. DONE-WHEN: docs describe the 'check' format + accept veto + 'lume check'; retro verdict recorded; command-check trust soft spot reviewed (kept open as discovery-triggered or resolved). ITERATION: closeout.

P5/L4 build step 1 of 4 - PYPROJECT + VERSION-CONSISTENCY CHECK. Add pyproject.toml (src-layout plugin/src/lume, project metadata, pytest config) and a small checker (e.g. a test or a tools/ script) asserting pyproject.version == plugin/.claude-plugin/plugin.json version. DONE-WHEN: pyproject present and parseable; 'python -m pytest' still discovers tests via it or conftest; consistency test fails on a deliberate version mismatch and passes when aligned; plugin.json remains the single source of record (pyproject reads/echoes it, no independent truth). ITERATION: execution.

P5/L4 build step 2 of 4 - CI WORKFLOW. Add .github/workflows/ci.yml running 'python -m pytest -q' on push + pull_request across python 3.10-3.13, plus the version-consistency check from step 1. DONE-WHEN: workflow is valid YAML and triggers on push/PR; matrix covers 3.10-3.13; the suite + consistency check run as steps; README notes CI status; (making it a REQUIRED check is the operator's branch-protection action, noted not done by the agent). ITERATION: execution.

P5/L4 build step 3 of 4 - TAG + RELEASE. Define and document the release flow: git tag vX.Y.Z where X.Y.Z == plugin.json version, plus a GitHub release; the tag/ref is the pinnable artifact (no built archive required for v0.1). DONE-WHEN: a documented release procedure exists; a check/script verifies tag == plugin.json version before release; an adopter-facing note shows how to pin via 'marketplace add' at a tag. (Cutting the actual v0.1.0 tag/release is an operator action, noted.) ITERATION: execution.

P5/L4 build step 4 of 4 - INSTALL VERIFICATION DOC + CLOSEOUT. Close the [U] live-install caveat with a documented, reproduced clean-state verification ('/plugin marketplace add' + '/plugin install'); assess the (refined) P5 done-when; closeout retro. DONE-WHEN: install-verification steps recorded with the result; P5 done-when assessed met/not with evidence; retro written; command-check trust + any new soft spots carried forward. ITERATION: closeout.

P2/L1 build step 1 of 4 - GAP SCHEMA + CAPTURE. Add a 'gap' entity schema (id, source, title, context, status open|acknowledged|resolved, created, resolution?) and 'lume gap add' writing gaps/<id>.json in the current repo (+ 'lume gap list'). DONE-WHEN: schema validates; gap add creates a well-formed record; list shows it; dedupe key (source,id) defined; unit tests cover add/list/validation. ITERATION: execution.

P2/L1 build step 2 of 4 - GIT SCAN/REACH. Add the ADOPTERS.md parser (repo URLs from the table) and the git reach: clone-if-absent/fetch-if-present under gitignored .lume/cache/adopters/<slug>/, ephemeral worktree at default ref, read gaps/*.json, prune worktree; unreachable repo => skip+report. DONE-WHEN: ADOPTERS table parsed; reach works against a LOCAL git repo fixture (clone+worktree+read); worktree pruned; failure is non-fatal; tests cover parse + reach + cleanup + missing-repo. ITERATION: execution.

P2/L1 build step 3 of 4 - INGEST + 'lume gap scan'. Wire scan -> read adopter gaps -> ingest OPEN ones into lume's gaps/ as 'acknowledged' (source+id), idempotent on rerun. DONE-WHEN: 'lume gap scan' ingests open gaps once; a second run adds nothing (idempotent); a CONTRACT TEST covers ingest + dedupe + status transition (open->acknowledged); operator can move ->resolved. ITERATION: execution.

P2/L1 build step 4 of 4 - DOCS + CLOSEOUT. Document the gap mechanism (README/skill + reconcile ADOPTERS.md to gaps/), assess P2 done-when, closeout retro. DONE-WHEN: docs describe gap add/scan/list + the gaps/ record; ADOPTERS.md reconciled; P2 done-when assessed met/not with evidence; retro records the deferred resolution round-trip + the git-cache trust/disk concerns as carry_forwards. ITERATION: closeout.

P4/L3 build step 1 of 4 - PARENT REF + SPAWN. Add optional 'parent' to schemas/workstream.json; Repository.create_workstream gains an optional parent (validated: parent exists, no self-parent); 'lume spawn <slug> "<title>"' creates a child of the -w target; Workstream exposes parent + a children-by-scan helper. DONE-WHEN: schema accepts parent + existing workstreams still validate; spawn creates a linked child; children(parent) returns it; spawning under a missing parent errors; tests cover spawn + link + validation. ITERATION: execution.

P4/L3 build step 2 of 4 - ROLLUP. Parent's 'lume status -w <parent>' detail lists child workstreams + each child's current phase; the cross-workstream queue ('lume status') NESTS children under their parent (indented), one level. DONE-WHEN: parent detail shows children+phase (human + --json); queue nests children; root-only repos render unchanged; existing snapshot/queue tests updated for the new shape; tests cover rollup. ITERATION: execution.

P4/L3 build step 3 of 4 - CLOSE/REOPEN GUARDS. 'lume close' on a parent with active (non-closed) children is REFUSED naming them; reopening a child whose parent is closed is REFUSED (reopen the parent first). DONE-WHEN: close-parent-with-active-children errors with the child list; close allowed once children closed; reopen-child-under-closed-parent errors; tests cover both guards + the allowed paths. ITERATION: execution.

P4/L3 build step 4 of 4 - DOCS + CLOSEOUT. Document spawn/rollup/guards (README/skill); assess P4 done-when; closeout retro; assess whether the workstream's whole objective (P1-P5 + P4) is now met and the workstream can close. DONE-WHEN: docs describe hierarchical workstreams; P4 done-when assessed; retro records derived-children cost + re-parent cycle-guard carry-forwards; objective assessment recorded. ITERATION: closeout.

workstream.title: Enable autonomous operation (tredl support) and delivery hardening
